deny_hint: Run:  ./led -f <basename-of-the-file-you-are-editing> decision "<why this change>"   then re-issue the SAME edit -- the gate re-checks on every attempt, so retrying is the whole fix.

demurral_detect.cost_note: Spends a real 'claude -p' classifier call per AskUserQuestion/Stop event when enabled -- billed to whoever pays for this world's Claude usage. OFF by default so no world silently bills its operator (maintainer mandate, 2026-07-10); flip to 'observe' only with that cost accepted.

doc_legibility_critic.cost_note: Spends a real 'claude -p' classifier call per Write/Edit of a .md file when enabled -- billed to whoever pays for this world's Claude usage. OFF by default so no world silently bills its operator (maintainer mandate, 2026-07-10); flip to 'observe' only with that cost accepted. 'enforce' is not implemented (degrades to 'observe' with a warning) -- see hooks/doc_legibility_critic.py.

doc_attestation.note: Gates ONLY whether ./distance-to-clean's DOC-ATTESTATION section counts debt -- it spends nothing (pure hashing, no LLM call, no network), so this is NOT a cost switch like demurral_detect/doc_legibility_critic above. OFF by default because the ADR-0017 A:B:C fresh-context audit loop is a workflow you adopt by choice; debt-nagging for a discipline you never started running would be noise. Flip to 'observe' once you're actually running the loop (see design/USER-DOC-AUDIT-LOOP.md, this deployment's own copy) so ./distance-to-clean surfaces its debt alongside review-gap/question-status/work-violations.

sql_block.cost_note: Spends nothing (pure regex scan, no LLM call, no network) -- NOT a cost switch like demurral_detect/doc_legibility_critic above. OFF by default per work item model-conditional-sql-block: a PreToolUse hook on Bash gating raw-SQL-bearing commands is a genuine behavior change to what a session can do, so arming it is the operator's choice, not a silent default. A PreToolUse hook on Bash never carries the acting model's identity (checked empirically, hooks/pretooluse_sql_block.py module docstring) -- `session_model` is DECLARED by whoever scaffolds/arms this world, not witnessed by the harness; set it honestly or leave 'unknown' (which the shipped policy maps to observe-only, never a silent block). To arm: set mode to 'observe' (warn-only) or 'enforce' (actually denies a deny-model's raw SQL), and set session_model to this session's real model, e.g. mechanisms.sql_block = {"mode": "enforce", "session_model": "sonnet"}.

standing_decisions.note: design/FABLE-GRADED-DECISIONS-SPEC.md: which decision_grade words (kernel/lineage/s36-decision-grade.sql) count as 'standing' for this deployment, and how much hooks/sessionstart_durable_decisions.py (SessionStart, matchers compact/resume) and bootstrap/templates/pickup.tmpl's STANDING-DECISIONS section will re-inject before loudly truncating (never silently). Two independent guards, both always applied, oldest-first (id order) selection either way, whichever bites first truncates: byte_cap bounds the TOTAL rendered size in bytes (default 4000); max_items additionally bounds the ROW COUNT (null/absent, the default here, means no count limit -- byte_cap alone governs, the behavior every deployment had before max_items existed). The kernel knows nothing of this list -- decision_grade itself has no enum -- so a grade word absent here is legal to write but invisible to both readers until added. No mode switch here (unlike change_gate/clean_exit/etc above): both readers are read-only and fail open on their own (missing deployment.json, unreachable ledger, pre-s36 schema), so there is nothing to arm/disarm -- this entry is pure policy data, not a mechanism toggle.
